mission.title: litellm: [Bug]: Claude Code not working with Vercel models due to "thinking" error
mission.description: [Bug]: Claude Code not working with Vercel models due to "thinking" error. This issue affects 30+ users.

steps.title: Identify litellm troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your litellm deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm -n litellm --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review litellm configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant litellm configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
kubectl get configmap -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=litellm
```
### Check for existing issues

### What happened?

I am using Anthropic Sonnet 4.5 through Vercel AI. I have this bug with Claude Code when thinking is enabled.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Bug]: Claude Code not working with Vercel models due to…
steps.description:
## Relevant issues
https://vercel.com/ai-gateway/models/claude-sonnet-4.5
## Pre-Submission checklist

- [see details](https://docs.litellm.ai/docs/extras/contributing_code)

## CI (LiteLLM team)

>

>
> - 50-55 passing tests: main is stable with minor issues.
> - 45-49 passing tests: acceptable
```yaml
`500 {"error":{"message":"Error calling litellm.acompletion for non-Anthropic model: litellm.UnsupportedParamsError: vercel_ai_gateway does not support       
     parameters: ['thinking'], for model=anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5. To drop these, 
     set 'litellm.drop_params=True' or for proxy:\n\n'litellm_settings:\n drop_params
      true'\n. \n If you want to use these params dynamically send                   
     allowed_openai_params=['thinking'] in your                                      
     request.","type":"None","param":"None","code":"500"}'`
```

steps.title: Confirm [Bug]: Claude Code not working with Vercel models… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm -n litellm --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n litellm --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with litellm installed or the issue environment reproducible.
